title: Organigram Q1 2023 Earnings Call Summary
text: Organigram Q1 2023 Earnings Call Summary. Financial Results - Revenue Growth: Gross revenue increased 37% to $60.9 million. Net revenue grew 43% to $43.3 million year-over-year. Profitability Metrics: Adjusted EBITDA: $5.6 million (positive for fourth consecutive quarter). Net income: $5.3 million vs. net loss of $1.3 million prior year. Adjusted gross margin: 30% of net revenues, up from 18% previously. Production Efficiency: Harvested approximately 22,000 kilos of flower, a 92% increase. Yield improved to 168 grams per plant, up 30% from 129 grams. Cost of cultivation reached historic lows. Market Position: Organigram maintained its No. 3 position among Canadian licensed producers, with leading positions including No. 2 in flower segment, No. 1 in milled flower by wide margin, and No. 1 in capsules nationally. CEO Beena Goldenberg noted that national market strength comes from focus on creating products that excite consumers. International Expansion: The company delivered $5.9 million in dried flower to Israel and Australia, representing 71% growth. A significant three-year agreement with Canndoc in Israel allows for 10,000 kilograms of supply. Strategic Initiatives: Introduced 17 new SKUs in Q1. Launched Holy Mountain and Wô Lá brands targeting value segments. Transitioned medical cannabis business to Shoppers Drug Mart platform.
